description_long: This is a robust wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held together by several thick, dark horizontal bands that appear to be made of wood or possibly metal. The wood grain is visibly weathered, giving the barrel a rustic, aged appearance suitable for outdoor or industrial urban settings. The top surface is flat and features a bold black skull-and-crossbones marking, suggesting it may be used for hazardous or restricted contents. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the lid appears to be securely fitted, making the barrel a closed receptacle. The overall craftsmanship and texture detail indicate it is designed to be both functional and visually striking in an urban environment.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark horizontal bands and a skull-and-crossbones marking on the lid, giving it a rugged, industrial look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat top with a centered skull-and-crossbones marking and the full curvature of the barrel.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the top marking and the layered banding around the barrel.
description_view_2: Side view with the marking partially visible and the wood grain texture prominent on the staves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the marking mostly visible and the banding structure clearly defined.
